MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Apr-13-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Zachariah D. Baker of K&L GATES LLP (415-882-8034), appearing for defendant / moving party Crane Co.
Patrick R. Campbell of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Omar Barstad, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT CRANE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

The Court already heard argument on the issues of threshold exposure to asbestos and substantial factor causation, however the Court put this motion back on calendar to give the parties the opportunity to address the motion for summary adjudication. Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Crane Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to Issues 1-4 and granted as to Issue 5. As to summary judgment and summary adjudication Issues 1-4, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant and that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that any exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to the risk of developing his asbestos-related disease. Plaintiff's responses to defendant's special discovery and Earl Daniel's deposition testimony preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to summary adjudication Issue 5 (punitive damages), defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiff failed to present clear and convincing evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. as such opportunity was not afforded to the parties at the April 9, 2015 hearing. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Erin F. Robinson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 12199 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Powell Company's motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's negligence and products liability causes of action are denied. Earl Daniel's declaration and the deposition testimony of William McClure and James Hengelhold create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (packing) attributable to defendant. Suzanne E. Rischman of SELMAN BREITMAN (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant / opposing party Lamons Gasket Company
(Refer to sign-in sheet for all other appearances.)

RULING:
PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Argued and the Court made the following ruling: Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). Trial date is April 13, 2015 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. The 120th day is April 17, 2015. The Trial Case Management Order dated May 7, 2014 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. Last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is April 9, 2015. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice dictated by the C.C.P. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Mr. Barstad's deposition will resume on 12/29/14 without further notice to defendants. Time to respond to written discovery is shortened to 20 days. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than March 5, 2015. Responses to the demand are due no later than March 12, 2015. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by April 7, 2015 at 3:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:00a.m. on April 9, 2015. Fact discovery cut-off date is March 27, 2015. Expert discovery cut-off date is April 10, 2015. No later than January 2, 2015 plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than January 2, 2015 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements will be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on March 18, 2015 at 1:30pm. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court by 3/16/15 to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on April 13, 2015 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by April 9, 2015 at 2:00p.m. Plaintiffs' counsel to prepare and submit an order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Holly Sayers of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13678 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
